themes.exampleQuote: Shout out to Bradley who made it so easy for me to schedule our upcoming event.
They let us taste around 5 different beers before we made a decision.
Tons of large tables, pool and darts.
QR codes, then table service, and now suddenly you have to order at the bar with no clear communication.
Absolutely love the lights and the live edge tables.

notable_quote: The key: I WILL BE HERE AGAIN.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers reliably call out the staff by name, particularly one floor manager who shows up in four separate reviews, and praise the tap variety and event-friendly layout; the single sustained complaint is about an inconsistent ordering system that left at least one table feeling ignored and walking out.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-one Pittsburgh creators filed 170,828 TikTok plays at Hop Farm Brewing this quarter, and Hop Farm itself posted none of it back, because the brewery has no TikTok account. That gap is the defining story of their spring: a Lawrenceville institution that the city is actively filming, narrating, and sharing, while the business watches from the sideline. Instagram tells a better story, twelve posts and four reels in thirty days puts them well above the family median, but the engagement rate of 0.47% suggests the account is broadcasting more than it is connecting. In the Pittsburgh Bars family, Hidden Harbor holds the top spot this issue on the strength of social momentum; Hop Farm's path to that position runs directly through converting TikTok creator energy into something the brewery controls.
tldr_read: Strong creator coverage and a perfect conversion path signal, but zero owned TikTok presence and soft review volume. Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Breweries.
tldr_meaning: The TikTok plays are already there and the Instagram cadence is improving; what separates Hop Farm from the top of the Bars family is whether the brewery starts claiming the platform where the city is already talking about it.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Number One Brewery, Fueled By Creators. The Brewery Has Never Posted Back.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok ground already yours
playbook.action: Open a business TikTok account and repost the 21 creator videos that generated 170,828 plays this quarter before that momentum cools.

playbook.headline: Fix the one friction point reviewers cite
playbook.action: Post a single clear sign at the entrance explaining the current ordering process; the one negative review in the sample is entirely about this confusion, not the beer or food.

playbook.headline: Push the photo catalog past 400
playbook.action: Visual Catalog sits above family median but has only six tagged categories; a monthly photo prompt or staff-shot series could close the gap before next quarter's count.
